headline: Super Micro Craters 27%, Dell Rises 5%: The AI Server Market Just Got a New Front-Runner
summary: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) stock plummeted 27% in Friday morning trading after federal prosecutors charged three company associates, including a co-founder, with illegally smuggling AI servers to China. Meanwhile, Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) stock is trading up 5% as investors rotate toward what they see as the cleaner, safer AI server play. Two companies competing in ... headline: Cramer: Dell crashed from $168 to $110 despite strong fundamentals
summary: Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) fell from $168 all the way down to $110, and Jim Cramer thinks the market got it completely wrong. On a recent Mad Money segment, Cramer argued the selloff was irrational, driven by macro sentiment rather than anything wrong with the business. The fundamentals, he said, never stopped being strong. headline: Super Micro co-founder's smuggling arrest, Planet Labs stock soars
summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Friday morning's trending tickers and stories. FedEx (FDX) earnings beat Wall Street's estimates on the top and bottom lines. The company also lifted its full-year guidance. The co-founder of Super Micro Computer (SMCI), Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, was arrested for allegedly smuggling Nvidia (NVDA)-powered servers to China. Planet Labs (PL) stock is skyrocketing after the company beat fourth quarter revenue expectations.

content.title: Super Micro co-founder's smuggling arrest, Planet Labs stock soars
content.description:
Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Friday morning's trending tickers and stories.

FedEx (FDX) earnings beat Wall Street's estimates on the top and bottom lines. The company also lifted its full-year guidance.

The co-founder of Super Micro Computer (SMCI), Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, was arrested for allegedly smuggling Nvidia (NVDA)-powered servers to China.

Planet Labs (PL) stock is skyrocketing after the company beat fourth quarter revenue expectations.
content.summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Friday morning's trending tickers and stories. FedEx (FDX) earnings beat Wall Street's estimates on the top and bottom lines. The company also lifted its full-year guidance. The co-founder of Super Micro Computer (SMCI), Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, was arrested for allegedly smuggling Nvidia (NVDA)-powered servers to China. Planet Labs (PL) stock is skyrocketing after the company beat fourth quarter revenue expectations.

headline: A Look At Dell Technologies (DELL) Valuation After Strong AI Server Demand And Capital Return Moves
summary: Dell Technologies (DELL) is back in focus after reporting strong fiscal-year revenue and earnings, tied closely to demand for its AI servers and new AI partnerships with NVIDIA and voice AI specialist Deepgram. See our latest analysis for Dell Technologies. The recent AI server momentum and new partnerships appear reflected in price action, with a 35.03% 1 month share price return and a 61.41% 1 year total shareholder return pointing to strong, building momentum rather than a short term...

headline: Super Micro Scandal Is a Big Win for Dell Stock
summary: Dell Technologies  stock surged Friday on news that had nothing to do with Dell.  The PC and server company got a boost after the Justice Department charged three individuals associated with  Super Micro Computer  Dell’s arch-rival, for allegedly skirting export-control laws by diverting $2.5 billion worth of U.S.-assembled servers to China.  One of the defendants is Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, Super Micro’s co-founder and senior vice president, as well as a board member.

content.title: Super Micro Scandal Is a Big Win for Dell Stock
content.summary: Dell Technologies  stock surged Friday on news that had nothing to do with Dell.  The PC and server company got a boost after the Justice Department charged three individuals associated with  Super Micro Computer  Dell’s arch-rival, for allegedly skirting export-control laws by diverting $2.5 billion worth of U.S.-assembled servers to China.  One of the defendants is Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, Super Micro’s co-founder and senior vice president, as well as a board member.

headline: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Paces Slump As Rates Spike; Super Micro Crashes, But Dell Jumps (Live Coverage)
summary: The Nasdaq Composite and other major stock indexes fell Friday as interest rates climbed.  Super Micro Computer was an early loser on the stock market today, but bad news for the server maker was good news for Dell.  Selling was concentrated in the Russell 2000 small-cap index and Nasdaq composite, which were both down around 1.3%.

headline: Super Micro shares plunge as US charges co-founder, 2 more for smuggling AI chips to China
summary: Super Micro shares sank 28% on Friday after U.S. prosecutors charged three people linked with the company, including ‌its co-founder, with helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of AI technology ‌to China.  U.S. prosecutors did not name Super Micro - a major AI server builder using Nvidia's chips - in ​the complaint.  The share drop could erase more than $5 billion from Super Micro's $18.49 billion market value, if they hold.

content.title: Super Micro shares plunge as US charges co-founder, 2 more for smuggling AI chips to China
content.summary: Super Micro shares sank 28% on Friday after U.S. prosecutors charged three people linked with the company, including ‌its co-founder, with helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of AI technology ‌to China.  U.S. prosecutors did not name Super Micro - a major AI server builder using Nvidia's chips - in ​the complaint.  The share drop could erase more than $5 billion from Super Micro's $18.49 billion market value, if they hold.
